title: Areas of shapes on grids
description: Practice finding the areas of triangles and quadrilaterals on grids.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the area of the triangle below?**  

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3aa609f3adddeae59a534e64a3c97978.png)

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{ units}^2$
hints:
We can enclose this triangle in a rectangle with area $7 \times 5 =35$.   
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a7de3994998fd6aefc7f297cb7bf5e20.png)

The areas of the three additional right triangles are:  

$\qquad \purple{\dfrac{4 \cdot 3}{2}} = \purple{6}$  

$\qquad \blue{\dfrac{2\cdot 7}{2}} = \blue{7}$  

$\qquad \red{\dfrac{3 \cdot 5}{2}} = \red{7.5}$.

The area of the original triangle can be found by subtracting the area of the additional right triangles from the area of the rectangle.  

$35-\purple{6}-\blue{7}-\red{7.5}=\green{14.5}$
The original triangle has an area of $14.5\text{ units}^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{ units}^2$
hints:
We can enclose this triangle in a square with area $5 \times 5 =25$.   
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/12231b44830df2a41a755a8f63d217db.png)

The areas of the three additional right triangles are:  

$\qquad \purple{\dfrac{4 \cdot 5}{2}} = \purple{10}$  

$\qquad \blue{\dfrac{3\cdot 5}{2}} = \blue{7.5}$  

$\qquad \red{\dfrac{1 \cdot 2}{2}} = \red{1}$.

The area of the original triangle can be found by subtracting the area of the additional right triangles from the area of the rectangle.  

$25-\purple{10}-\blue{7.5}-\red{1}=\green{6.5}$
The original triangle has an area of $6.5\text{ units}^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the area of the triangle below?**  

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bf1d8bec52df5c939c26c36d5cfcfd41.png)

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{ units}^2$
hints:
We can enclose this triangle in a rectangle  with area $4 \times 5 =20$.   
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cc0d8189a34ccf83808dd4eafb85e344.png)

There are three additional triangles and a square.  

The areas of the three additional right triangles are:  

$\qquad \purple{\dfrac{4 \cdot 1}{2}} = \purple{2}$  

$\qquad \blue{\dfrac{5\cdot 4}{2}} = \blue{10}$  

$\qquad \red{\dfrac{3 \cdot 1}{2}} = \red{1.5}$.

The area of the square is: 

$\qquad \gray 1\cdot \gray 1=1$.

The area of the original triangle can be found by subtracting the area of the additional right triangles and square from the area of the rectangle.  

$20-\purple{2}-\blue{10}-\red{1.5}-\gray 1=\green{5.5}$
The original triangle has an area of $\,5.5\text{ units}^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:

There are three additional triangles and a rectangle.  

The areas of the three additional right triangles are:  

$\qquad \purple{\dfrac{4 \cdot 3}{2}} = \purple{6}$  

$\qquad \blue{\dfrac{4\cdot 1}{2}} = \blue{2}$  

$\qquad \red{\dfrac{7 \cdot 5}{2}} = \red{17.5}$.

The area of the rectangle is: 

$\qquad \gray1\cdot \gray3=\gray3$.

The area of the original triangle can be found by subtracting the area of the additional right triangles and rectangle from the area of the large rectangle.  

$35-\purple{6}-\blue{2}-\red{17.5}-\gray 3=\green{6.5}$
The original triangle has an area of $\,6.5\text{ units}^2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the area of the polygon below?**  

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fbc8a797f4c53d4e0d604cfbd51465bb.png)

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{ units}^2$
hints:
We can enclose this polygon in a rectangle with area $7 \times 6 =42$.   

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aaaee8d4f849e6eab1825ea700bc191f.png)

The areas of the four right triangles are:  

$\qquad \purple{\dfrac{6 \cdot 2}{2}} = \purple{6}$  

$\qquad \pink{\dfrac{4 \cdot1}{2}} = \pink{2}$  

$\qquad \green{\dfrac{6 \cdot1}{2}} = \green{3}$  

$\qquad \red{\dfrac{5 \cdot 1}{2}} = \red{2.5}$

The area of the original polygon can be found by subtracting the area of the right triangles from the area of the rectangle.  

$42-\purple{6}-\pink2-\green{3}-\red {2.5}=\blue{28.5}$
**The polygon has an area of $28.5 \text{ units}^2$.**

$\qquad \green{\dfrac{5 \cdot1}{2}} = \green{2.5}$  

$\qquad \red{\dfrac{5 \cdot 1}{2}} = \red{2.5}$

The area of the original polygon can be found by subtracting the area of the right triangles from the area of the rectangle.  

$42-\purple{6}-\pink4-\green{2.5}-\red {2.5}=\blue{27}$
**The polygon has an area of $27 \text{ units}^2$.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the area of the polygon below?**  

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6290893abe03ad7d7f3c4e4fbc22c215.png)

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{ units}^2$
hints:
We can enclose this polygon in a square with area $7 \times 7 =49$.   
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c80dee9a947a8f925c3f9ff9d8aef670.png)

The areas of the four right triangles are:  

$\qquad \purple{\dfrac{6 \cdot 3}{2}} = \purple9$  

$\qquad \pink{\dfrac{6 \cdot4}{2}} = \pink{12}$  

$\qquad \green{\dfrac{1 \cdot1}{2}} = \green{0.5}$  

$\qquad \red{\dfrac{6 \cdot 1}{2}} = \red{3}$

The area of the original polygon can be found by subtracting the area of the right triangles from the area of the square.  

$49-\purple9-\pink{12}-\green{0.5}-\red {3}=\blue{24.5}$
**The polygon has an area of $24.5 \text{ units}^2$.**
